name: Greg Abbott
bio: Greg Abbott has served as the 48th Governor of Texas since January 20, 2015, and is the longest-serving incumbent governor in the United States as of 2025. He previously served as Texas Attorney General from 2002 to 2015 and as a Justice on the Texas Supreme Court from 1996 to 2001.

stances.position_label: Growth through deregulation and tax cuts
stances.summary: Abbott consistently advocates for economic growth through deregulation, tax cuts, and business-friendly policies. He created the Small Business Freedom Council to slash state agency rules and fees, and emphasizes keeping state spending below constitutional limits.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: That's why I created the Small Business Freedom Council to require all state agencies to slash rules, fees, and regulations. It's why we will keep state spending below constitutional limits and why we must cut your taxes.

stances.position_label: Reduce spending and regulation to lower prices
stances.summary: Abbott addresses cost-of-living concerns through deregulation, tax relief, and reducing government-imposed costs rather than direct intervention. His housing affordability approach focuses on cutting regulations and speeding up permitting.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: We must also make housing more affordable. To do that, we need to make it easier to build, slash regulations, and speed up permitting.

stances.summary: Abbott has made property tax reduction a centerpiece of his governance, signing over $18 billion in property tax cuts in 2023 and seeking at least $10 billion more. He advocates for eliminating school property taxes for homeowners and raising business personal property exemptions.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: I want at least $10 billion in new property tax relief... I recommend the 89th Legislature return $10 billion to taxpayers this session by way of property tax relief.

sources.title: Proposed State Budget for FY 2026-2027
sources.quote: I further recommend raising the Business Personal Property exemption to $100,000, to support more small businesses.

sources.title: Governor Abbott: Power to the Texas Taxpayer
sources.quote: Governor Abbott will – through a constitutional amendment – empower Texans to vote to eliminate school district property taxes on homeowners

stances.summary: Abbott focuses on job creation through business-friendly policies, emphasizing career and technical education for workforce development rather than labor standards mandates. He highlights Texas adding over 2.5 million jobs since he became governor through low taxes and deregulation.

sources.title: Proposed State Budget for FY 2026-2027
sources.quote: Since I've been Governor, Texas has added over 2.5 million new jobs. We will continue to expand job growth through our business-friendly policies, low taxes, and strategic investments in innovation and education.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: That's why I am making life-changing career training an emergency item.

stances.position_label: Deregulate: cut zoning rules so private builders add supply
stances.summary: Abbott explicitly advocates for housing affordability through deregulation, cutting regulations, and speeding up permitting to make it easier to build. He also proposes a one-year tax exemption on home improvements.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: We must also make housing more affordable. To do that, we need to make it easier to build, slash regulations, and speed up permitting. And to make your current home more affordable, we should offer a one-year tax exemption on home improvements, like heating and air conditioning.

stances.position_label: Expand domestic oil, gas, and coal production; includes nuclear
stances.summary: Abbott emphasizes expanding traditional energy sources including oil and gas, while also supporting nuclear power. He created the Texas Energy Fund with $5 billion investment and advocates for Texas to lead a 'nuclear power renaissance.'

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: We must add more power this session to better fortify our grid. It's also time for Texas to lead a nuclear power renaissance in the United States.

sources.title: Proposed State Budget for FY 2026-2027
sources.quote: With the largest surplus in Texas history last session, in addition to substantial tax relief, additional historic and generational investments were made, including $5 billion for the Texas Energy Fund

stances.position_label: Cut spending significantly; keep below constitutional limits
stances.summary: Abbott consistently advocates for limiting state spending below constitutional limits, cutting taxes, and requiring voter approval for local spending increases. He proposes tying local government spending to population plus inflation or 3.5%.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: It's why we will keep state spending below constitutional limits and why we must cut your taxes.

sources.title: Governor Abbott: Power to the Texas Taxpayer
sources.quote: Governor Abbott will limit local government spending to population plus inflation or 3.5% (the lesser of the two) to ensure fiscally-responsible governance.

stances.position_label: State investment with some private elements; major water investment
stances.summary: Abbott supports substantial state infrastructure investment, particularly for water and energy, while also emphasizing public-private partnerships. He declared water infrastructure an emergency item with the largest investment in Texas history, and previously invested $1.5 billion in broadband.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: Working with Senator Perry and Representative Cody Harris, we will put Texas on a path to have plenty of water for the next 50 years. We will make the largest investment in water in the history of Texas.

sources.title: Proposed State Budget for FY 2026-2027
sources.quote: With the largest surplus in Texas history last session, in addition to substantial tax relief, additional historic and generational investments were made, including...$1.5 billion for broadband infrastructure funding

stances.position_label: Cut regulations and taxes on small businesses
stances.summary: Abbott prioritizes reducing regulatory and tax burdens on small businesses. He created the Small Business Freedom Council to slash rules and fees, and proposes raising the Business Personal Property exemption from $2,500 to $100,000.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Delivers 2025 State Of The State Address
sources.quote: That's why I created the Small Business Freedom Council to require all state agencies to slash rules, fees, and regulations.

sources.title: Proposed State Budget for FY 2026-2027
sources.quote: Texas imposes property tax on business personal property (BPP) with a value of more than $2,500. Significantly increasing the BPP exemption will be a great benefit to Texas' small businesses, the lifeblood of our state's economy. I further recommend raising the Business Personal Property exemption to $100,000

stances.position_label: Market-based, state flexibility; oppose federal single-payer
stances.summary: Abbott consistently supports reducing federal government role in healthcare, advocating for state flexibility through block grants and patient-driven market solutions. He endorsed the Graham-Cassidy bill to give states more control and opposes 'Bernie Sanders style' single-payer systems.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Statement On Graham-Cassidy Health Care Bill
sources.quote: The Graham-Cassidy bill aspires to the goal of reducing the role of the federal government in health care decisions and giving states more flexibility to help their citizens make health care decisions better tailored to their unique needs. I trust Texans know better how to address their health care needs than bureaucrats in Washington.

sources.title: Greg Abbott's Healthy Texans Plan
sources.quote: Texas should request the federal government provide Medicaid funds in the form of a block grant.

stances.position_label: Strong school choice advocate; ESAs and parental control
stances.summary: Abbott is a leading proponent of school choice, having signed Texas's landmark Education Savings Account program in 2025. He campaigned on 'education freedom,' supports diverting public funds to private options through ESAs, and emphasizes parental control over education decisions.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Signs Landmark School Choice Legislation Into Law
sources.quote: When I ran for re-election in 2022, I promised Texans that we will bring education freedom to every Texas family. Today, Texas delivers on that promise. I am signing this law that will ensure Texas families, whose children can no longer be served by the public school assigned to them, have the choice to take their money and find the school that is right for them.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Signs Record Public Education Funding, Teacher Pay Raise Into Law
sources.quote: School choice, paired with the House's additional proposal to initiate the largest increase in public education funding in Texas history, will elevate the overall quality of our educational system

stances.position_label: Block grants, state control; restrain federal program growth
stances.summary: Abbott advocates for converting Medicaid to block grants to give states more discretion, arguing this enables policy innovation and cost savings. His 2014 policy platform explicitly called for requesting federal Medicaid funds as block grants, and he criticized Obamacare's expansion approach.

sources.title: Greg Abbott's Healthy Texans Plan
sources.quote: Texas should request the federal government provide Medicaid funds in the form of a block grant. Recent experience with block grants demonstrates clearly that states can pioneer new and effective policies and use funds more efficiently if they have more discretion over how to use Medicaid funding.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Statement On Graham-Cassidy Health Care Bill
sources.quote: The Graham-Cassidy bill aspires to the goal of reducing the role of the federal government in health care decisions and giving states more flexibility

stances.summary: Abbott signed Texas Senate Bill 8, which prohibits abortion after detection of a fetal heartbeat (approximately 6 weeks) and authorizes private citizens to sue abortion providers. The law contains only narrow exceptions and represents one of the most restrictive abortion policies in the nation.

sources.title: 87(R) SB 8 - Enrolled version
sources.quote: relating to abortion, including abortions after detection of an unborn child's heartbeat; authorizing a private civil right of action.

stances.summary: Governor Abbott has signed multiple bills that criminalize illegal entry and re-entry, mandate prison sentences for smuggling, and expand physical barriers and prosecutorial tools against unauthorized migrants.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Signs Historic Border Security Measures In Brownsville
sources.quote: Senate Bill 4 … creates a criminal offense for illegal entry into this state from a foreign nation … and penalizes offenders with up to 20 years in prison.

stances.summary: Abbott’s 2024 “Protecting Communities, Not Criminals” plan proposes automatic denial of bail for undocumented immigrants charged with felonies, expanded task-force presence, and removal of district attorneys who decline to prosecute certain crimes.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Rolls Out 'Protecting Communities, Not Criminals' Public Safety Policy Initiatives
sources.quote: Automatically deny bail for illegal immigrants charged with felony crimes … Expand the Department of Public Safety Repeat Offender Taskforce … Impeach rogue district attorneys who fail to enforce the law.

stances.summary: Abbott has championed Texas as 'the epicenter of AI development' and supported Google's $40 billion AI infrastructure investment, while also signing the Texas Responsible Artificial Intelligence Governance Act (HB 149), which imposes civil penalties for AI systems that infringe constitutional rights, discriminate unlawfully, or manipulate human behavior. He has simultaneously called for strict regulation of data centers powering AI, including requirements for self-funded power generation, water recycling, and repeal of tax exemptions.

sources.title: 89(R) HB 149 - Enrolled version
sources.quote: A person may not develop or deploy an artificial intelligence system with the sole intent for the artificial intelligence system to infringe, restrict, or otherwise impair an individual's rights guaranteed under the United States Constitution

sources.title: Greg Abbott recommends data center industry crack down
sources.quote: requiring new facilities to add power generation to the state's power grid...repealing data center sales tax exemptions

sources.title: Abbott calls for ban on data center development in rural Texas
sources.quote: I made clear already: Any AI data center even thinking about coming here — they got to bring their own money, bring their own power, reuse their own water

stances.position_label: National security-focused; limited consumer privacy protections
stances.summary: Abbott has prioritized cybersecurity and data protection from foreign adversaries through the Texas Cyber Command and expanded prohibited technologies lists targeting Chinese-affiliated AI and hardware. However, his approach emphasizes state-led national security measures over comprehensive consumer privacy rights, with HB 149 specifically stating it does not provide a private right of action for AI violations, reserving enforcement exclusively to the Attorney General.

sources.title: Governor Abbott Updates Texas' Prohibited Technologies List
sources.quote: Hostile adversaries harvest user data through AI and other applications and hardware to exploit, manipulate, and violate users and put them at extreme risk

sources.title: 89(R) HB 149 - Enrolled version
sources.quote: This chapter does not provide a basis for, and is not subject to, a private right of action for a violation of this chapter or any other law. The attorney general has exclusive authority to enforce this chapter

contradictions.description: The summary says Abbott supports expanding oil and gas production and nuclear power, which is broadly pro-fossil-fuel and pro-nuclear; this does not contradict the other stances.

The summary combines substantial state infrastructure spending with public-private partnerships; this is not inherently contradictory to the rest of Abbott's low-tax, limited-spending positions because it is a targeted investment stance.
The summary mixes pro-innovation support for AI with sector-specific regulation, which is a nuanced mixed stance rather than a contradiction.

The summary emphasizes state-led cybersecurity and national security controls rather than broad consumer privacy rights, which is consistent with the scalar and not a contradiction with the other stances.

qualitative.summary: No criminal indictments, impeachment proceedings, or formal ethics violations have been filed against Abbott. A 2013 Houston Chronicle report raised questions about donor ties to a state cancer-research agency, but no enforcement action followed.

sources.title: Greg Abbott

qualitative.summary: Polling by the Texas Politics Project shows Abbott’s approval has hovered near 50 % in 2026, indicating a polarized but not overwhelmingly negative electorate. No independent audits of constituent-services caseloads are publicly available.

sources.title: Greg Abbott Approval (June 2026)
Greg Abbott Approval (April 2026)

qualitative.summary: Abbott releases statutorily required personal financial statements but has resisted FOIA requests when communications involve deliberative processes; press access was restricted during parts of the 2021 power-crisis briefings. No centralized public database of his office’s open-records compliance exists.

sources.title: Greg Abbott

qualitative.summary: Abbott has held continuously higher state offices since 1996: Texas Supreme Court justice (1996-2001), attorney general (2002-2015), and governor (2015-present), making him the longest-serving incumbent U.S. governor. Public records list 50 vetoed bills and multiple special-session agendas demonstrating sustained legislative engagement.

sources.title: Greg Abbott

promise_record.promise: Seek federal approval to receive Medicaid aid via block grants

promise_source.title: Fund Medicaid by block grants: Greg Abbott's vow to seek block-grant Medicaid funding unfulfilled
promise_source.quote: Greg Abbott stumped for governor by saying the state should seek federal approval to receive Medicaid aid via block grants with the state, he said, better managing how such money is spent.

promise_record.action: Took no action to seek block-grant approval; no proposal submitted to federal officials

action_sources.title: Fund Medicaid by block grants: Greg Abbott's vow to seek block-grant Medicaid funding unfulfilled

promise_record.explanation: Abbott campaigned on seeking Medicaid block grants but 15 months into his governorship had not submitted any proposal to federal officials, and no legislative action was taken to enable such a request.

promise_record.promise: Sign a law making it legal to openly carry handguns in public

promise_source.title: Legalize openly carrying handguns in public: Abbott fulfills promise to support law permitting handguns to be openly carried
promise_source.quote: At a Corpus Christi stop in 2013, then-candidate Abbott made it clear he would be fine with Texas lawmakers letting Texans openly carry handguns in public.

promise_record.action: Signed HB 910 into law, allowing licensed Texans to openly carry handguns in shoulder or belt holsters

action_sources.title: Legalize openly carrying handguns in public: Abbott fulfills promise to support law permitting handguns to be openly carried

promise_record.explanation: Abbott signed HB 910 on June 13, 2015, fulfilling his campaign pledge to make Texas the 45th state allowing open carry of handguns.

promise_record.promise: Repeal limits on students taking online classes through the Texas Virtual School Network

promise_source.title: Repeal limits on students taking online classes: Abbott promise to repeal limits on online courses goes unfulfilled
promise_source.quote: In order to guarantee that digital learning is available to all students at any time, Texas must take steps to make sure the TxVSN is robust, accessible, and transparent.

promise_record.action: Legislation was introduced but failed to pass; no law repealing limits was enacted

action_sources.title: Repeal limits on students taking online classes: Abbott promise to repeal limits on online courses goes unfulfilled

promise_record.explanation: Though Abbott supported the idea, legislation to remove limits on online courses passed committee in 2015 but never reached the full Senate, and no similar bill was introduced in 2017.

promise_record.promise: Bar state government from setting up an Obamacare marketplace/exchange

promise_source.title: Bar state government from setting up an Obamacare marketplace/exchange: Abbott call to bar state government from setting up Obamacare exchange not passed into law
promise_source.quote: As a candidate, Greg Abbott made it clear he wanted to forbid state government from setting up a marketplace (or exchange) enabling Texans to buy health insurance in keeping with the 2010 Obamacare law.

promise_record.action: No legislation barring a state exchange was enacted; one related bill died in committee

action_sources.title: Bar state government from setting up an Obamacare marketplace/exchange: Abbott call to bar state government from setting up Obamacare exchange not passed into law

promise_record.explanation: Abbott advocated for barring a state Obamacare exchange but the 2015 legislature took no final action, with the relevant bill dying in committee without reaching a floor vote.

source.title: Abbott enters Texas governor's race with $106 million war chest

correlations.position_or_vote: Expand domestic oil, gas, and coal production; includes nuclear
correlations.note: Kelcy Warren is Executive Chairman of Energy Transfer, one of the largest midstream energy companies in the U.S.; Abbott's documented energy position aligns with expanding fossil fuel production, though public records do not establish this donation caused any specific policy decision.

sources.title: Greg Abbott - Texas Candidate - Contributions

correlations.position_or_vote: Expand domestic oil, gas, and coal production; includes nuclear
correlations.note: Anwar is a Midland oil executive whom Abbott appointed to the Texas Higher Education Coordinating Board; Abbott's energy expansion position correlates with Anwar's industry interests, though no causal relationship is documented.

sources.title: Abbott enters Texas governor's race with $106 million war chest

correlations.position_or_vote: Cut regulations and taxes on small businesses
correlations.note: Adelson is a casino magnate whose business interests include gaming and hospitality; Abbott's position on reducing business regulations correlates with industry preferences, though no specific vote or action tied to this donation is documented.

sources.title: Abbott enters Texas governor's race with $106 million war chest

correlations.donor: Black Mountain Power LLC / Data center development
correlations.position_or_vote: State investment with some private elements; major water investment
correlations.note: Black Mountain Power is developing data centers requiring substantial energy and water infrastructure; Abbott's infrastructure position emphasizing state investment with private elements correlates with the company's project needs near Fort Worth.

sources.title: Greg Abbott has $106M as he seeks record fourth term

correlations.note: Gebbia co-founded Airbnb and has tech sector interests; Abbott's mixed position on technology—pro-innovation with sector-specific regulation—correlates with tech industry preferences for limited regulatory frameworks.

sources.title: Greg Abbott - Texas Candidate - Contributions

correlations.note: Wood founded Roku, a streaming media company; Abbott's technology position emphasizing innovation with targeted regulation correlates with streaming industry interests in maintaining open platforms.

sources.title: Greg Abbott - Texas Candidate - Contributions
